1.arabic: بسم الله الرحمن الرحيم والطور
1.english: By the mount
1.transliteration: Wat-Toor

1.arabic: وكتاب مسطور
1.english: And [by] a Book inscribed
1.transliteration: Wa kitaabim mastoor

1.arabic: في رق منشور
1.english: In parchment spread open
1.transliteration: Fee raqqim manshoor

1.arabic: والبيت المعمور
1.english: And [by] the frequented House
1.transliteration: Wal baitil ma'moor

1.arabic: والسقف المرفوع
1.english: And [by] the heaven raised high
1.transliteration: Wassaqfil marfoo'

1.arabic: والبحر المسجور
1.english: And [by] the sea filled [with fire],
1.transliteration: Wal bahril masjoor

1.arabic: إن عذاب ربك لواقع
1.english: Indeed, the punishment of your Lord will occur.
1.transliteration: Inna 'azaaba Rabbika lawaaqi'

1.arabic: ما له من دافع
1.english: Of it there is no preventer.
1.transliteration: Maa lahoo min daafi'

1.arabic: يوم تمور السماء مورا
1.english: On the Day the heaven will sway with circular motion
1.transliteration: Yawma tamoorus samaaa'u mawraa

1.arabic: وتسير الجبال سيرا
1.english: And the mountains will pass on, departing -
1.transliteration: Wa taseerul jibaalu sairaa

1.arabic: فويل يومئذ للمكذبين
1.english: Then woe, that Day, to the deniers,
1.transliteration: Fawailuny yawma 'izil lil mukaazzibeen

1.arabic: الذين هم في خوض يلعبون
1.english: Who are in [empty] discourse amusing themselves.
1.transliteration: Allazeena hum fee khawdiny yal'aboon

1.arabic: يوم يدعون إلى نار جهنم دعا
1.english: The Day they are thrust toward the fire of Hell with a [violent] thrust, [its angels will say],
1.transliteration: Yawma yuda'-'oona ilaa naari jahannama da'-'aa

1.arabic: هذه النار التي كنتم بها تكذبون
1.english: "This is the Fire which you used to deny.
1.transliteration: Haazihin naarul latee kuntum bihaa tukazziboon

1.arabic: أفسحر هذا أم أنتم لا تبصرون
1.english: Then is this magic, or do you not see?
1.transliteration: Afasihrun haazaaaa am antum laa tubsiroon

1.arabic: اصلوها فاصبروا أو لا تصبروا سواء عليكم ۖ إنما تجزون ما كنتم تعملون
1.english: [Enter to] burn therein; then be patient or impatient - it is all the same for you. You are only being recompensed [for] what you used to do."
1.transliteration: Islawhaa fasbirooo aw laa tasbiroo sawaaa'un 'alaikum innamaa tujzawna maa kuntum ta'maloon

1.arabic: إن المتقين في جنات ونعيم
1.english: Indeed, the righteous will be in gardens and pleasure,
1.transliteration: Innal muttaqeena fee jannaatinw wa na'eem

1.arabic: فاكهين بما آتاهم ربهم ووقاهم ربهم عذاب الجحيم
1.english: Enjoying what their Lord has given them, and their Lord protected them from the punishment of Hellfire.
1.transliteration: Faakiheena bimaaa aataahum rabbuhum wa waqaahum rabbuhum 'azaabal jaheem

1.arabic: كلوا واشربوا هنيئا بما كنتم تعملون
1.english: [They will be told], "Eat and drink in satisfaction for what you used to do."
1.transliteration: Kuloo washraboo haneee 'am bimaa kuntum ta'maloon

1.arabic: متكئين على سرر مصفوفة ۖ وزوجناهم بحور عين
1.english: They will be reclining on thrones lined up, and We will marry them to fair women with large, [beautiful] eyes.
1.transliteration: Muttaki'eena 'alaa sururim masfoofatinw wa zawwaj naahum bihoorin 'een

1.arabic: والذين آمنوا واتبعتهم ذريتهم بإيمان ألحقنا بهم ذريتهم وما ألتناهم من عملهم من شيء ۚ كل امرئ بما كسب رهين
1.english: And those who believed and whose descendants followed them in faith - We will join with them their descendants, and We will not deprive them of anything of their deeds. Every person, for what he earned, is retained.
1.transliteration: Wallazeena aamanoo wattaba'at hum zurriyyatuhum bieemaanin alhaqnaa bihim zurriyyatahum wa maaa alatnaahum min 'amalihim min shai'; kullum ri'im bimaa kasaba raheen

1.arabic: وأمددناهم بفاكهة ولحم مما يشتهون
1.english: And We will provide them with fruit and meat from whatever they desire.
1.transliteration: Wa amdadnaahum bifaa kihatinw wa lahmim mimmaa yashtahoon

1.arabic: يتنازعون فيها كأسا لا لغو فيها ولا تأثيم
1.english: They will exchange with one another a cup [of wine] wherein [results] no ill speech or commission of sin.
1.transliteration: Yatanaaza'oona feehaa kaasal laa laghwun feehaa wa laa taaseem

1.arabic: ۞ ويطوف عليهم غلمان لهم كأنهم لؤلؤ مكنون
1.english: There will circulate among them [servant] boys [especially] for them, as if they were pearls well-protected.
1.transliteration: Wa yatoofu 'alaihim ghilmaanul lahum ka annahum lu'lu'um maknoon

1.arabic: وأقبل بعضهم على بعض يتساءلون
1.english: And they will approach one another, inquiring of each other.
1.transliteration: Wa aqbala ba'duhum 'alaa ba'diny yatasaaa'aloon

1.arabic: قالوا إنا كنا قبل في أهلنا مشفقين
1.english: They will say, "Indeed, we were previously among our people fearful [of displeasing Allah].
1.transliteration: Qaalooo innaa kunnaa qablu feee ahlinaa mushfiqeen

1.arabic: فمن الله علينا ووقانا عذاب السموم
1.english: So Allah conferred favor upon us and protected us from the punishment of the Scorching Fire.
1.transliteration: Famannnal laahu 'alainaa wa waqaanaa 'azaabas samoom

1.arabic: إنا كنا من قبل ندعوه ۖ إنه هو البر الرحيم
1.english: Indeed, we used to supplicate Him before. Indeed, it is He who is the Beneficent, the Merciful."
1.transliteration: Innaa kunnaa min qablu nad'oohu innahoo huwal barrur raheem

1.arabic: فذكر فما أنت بنعمت ربك بكاهن ولا مجنون
1.english: So remind [O Muhammad], for you are not, by the favor of your Lord, a soothsayer or a madman.
1.transliteration: Fazakkir famaaa anta bini'mati rabbika bikaahininw wa laa majnoon

1.arabic: أم يقولون شاعر نتربص به ريب المنون
1.english: Or do they say [of you], "A poet for whom we await a misfortune of time?"
1.transliteration: Am yaqooloona shaa'irun natarabbasu bihee raibal manoon

1.arabic: قل تربصوا فإني معكم من المتربصين
1.english: Say, "Wait, for indeed I am, with you, among the waiters."
1.transliteration: Qul tarabbasoo fa innee ma'akum minal mutarabbiseen

1.arabic: أم تأمرهم أحلامهم بهذا ۚ أم هم قوم طاغون
1.english: Or do their minds command them to [say] this, or are they a transgressing people?
1.transliteration: Am taamuruhum ahlaamuhum bihaazaaa am hum qawmun taaghoon

1.arabic: أم يقولون تقوله ۚ بل لا يؤمنون
1.english: Or do they say, "He has made it up"? Rather, they do not believe.
1.transliteration: Am yaqooloona taqawwalah; bal laa yu'minoon

1.arabic: فليأتوا بحديث مثله إن كانوا صادقين
1.english: Then let them produce a statement like it, if they should be truthful.
1.transliteration: Falyaatoo bihadeesim misliheee in kaanoo saadiqeen

1.arabic: أم خلقوا من غير شيء أم هم الخالقون
1.english: Or were they created by nothing, or were they the creators [of themselves]?
1.transliteration: Am khuliqoo min ghairi shai'in am humul khaaliqoon

1.arabic: أم خلقوا السماوات والأرض ۚ بل لا يوقنون
1.english: Or did they create the heavens and the earth? Rather, they are not certain.
1.transliteration: Am khalaqus samaawaati wal ard; bal laa yooqinoon

1.arabic: أم عندهم خزائن ربك أم هم المصيطرون
1.english: Or have they the depositories [containing the provision] of your Lord? Or are they the controllers [of them]?
1.transliteration: Am'indahum khazaaa'inu rabbika am humul musaitiroon

1.arabic: أم لهم سلم يستمعون فيه ۖ فليأت مستمعهم بسلطان مبين
1.english: Or have they a stairway [into the heaven] upon which they listen? Then let their listener produce a clear authority.
1.transliteration: Am lahum sullamuny yastami'oona feehi falyaati mustami'uhum bisultaanim mubeen

1.arabic: أم له البنات ولكم البنون
1.english: Or has He daughters while you have sons?
1.transliteration: Am lahul banaatu wa lakumul banoon

1.arabic: أم تسألهم أجرا فهم من مغرم مثقلون
1.english: Or do you, [O Muhammad], ask of them a payment, so they are by debt burdened down?
1.transliteration: Am tas'aluhum ajran fahum mim maghramim musqaloon

1.arabic: أم عندهم الغيب فهم يكتبون
1.english: Or have they [knowledge of] the unseen, so they write [it] down?
1.transliteration: Am 'indahumul ghaibu fahum yaktuboon

1.arabic: أم يريدون كيدا ۖ فالذين كفروا هم المكيدون
1.english: Or do they intend a plan? But those who disbelieve - they are the object of a plan.
1.transliteration: Am yureedoona kaidan fallazeena kafaroo humul makeedoon

1.arabic: أم لهم إله غير الله ۚ سبحان الله عما يشركون
1.english: Or have they a deity other than Allah? Exalted is Allah above whatever they associate with Him.
1.transliteration: Am lahum ilaahun ghairul laa; subhaanal laahi 'ammaa yushrikoon

1.arabic: وإن يروا كسفا من السماء ساقطا يقولوا سحاب مركوم
1.english: And if they were to see a fragment from the sky falling, they would say, "[It is merely] clouds heaped up."
1.transliteration: Wa iny yaraw kisfam minas samaaa'i saaqitany yaqooloo sahaabum markoom

1.arabic: فذرهم حتى يلاقوا يومهم الذي فيه يصعقون
1.english: So leave them until they meet their Day in which they will be struck insensible -
1.transliteration: Fazarhum hatta yulaaqoo yawmahumul lazee feehi yus'aqoon

1.arabic: يوم لا يغني عنهم كيدهم شيئا ولا هم ينصرون
1.english: The Day their plan will not avail them at all, nor will they be helped.
1.transliteration: Yawma laa yughnee 'anhum kaidumhum shai'anw wa laa hum yunsaroon

1.arabic: وإن للذين ظلموا عذابا دون ذلك ولكن أكثرهم لا يعلمون
1.english: And indeed, for those who have wronged is a punishment before that, but most of them do not know.
1.transliteration: Wa inna lillazeena zalamoo 'azaaban doona zalika wa laakinna aksarahum laa ya'lamoon

1.arabic: واصبر لحكم ربك فإنك بأعيننا ۖ وسبح بحمد ربك حين تقوم
1.english: And be patient, [O Muhammad], for the decision of your Lord, for indeed, you are in Our eyes. And exalt [Allah] with praise of your Lord when you arise.
1.transliteration: Wasbir lihukmi rabbika fa innaka bi-a'yuninaa wa sabbih bihamdi rabbika heena taqoom

1.arabic: ومن الليل فسبحه وإدبار النجوم
1.english: And in a part of the night exalt Him and after [the setting of] the stars.
1.transliteration: Wa minal laili fasabbihhu wa idbaaran nujoom
